articles.title: Dozens of states could face new costs because of high error rates in SNAP food aid
articles.description:
More than 37 million people nationwide received SNAP benefits in March, according to preliminary USDA figures. That's down nearly 5 million people — over 11% — from a year earlier.

The post Dozens of states could face new costs because of high error rates in SNAP food aid appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: SNAP benefits recipients dropped to 37 million in March, a decrease of 5 million from last year.
High error rates in SNAP could lead to increased costs for Massachusetts and other states.
Boston residents may face disruptions in food aid services due to potential funding issues.

articles.title: Vermont is the first state to ban paraquat, a weed killer linked to Parkinson’s disease
articles.description:
“Vermont took the step to be the leader in this, and that’s significant because it shifts the conversation,” said Dan Feehan, with The Michael J. Fox Foundation, the world’s largest nonprofit funder of Parkinson’s research.

The post Vermont is the first state to ban paraquat, a weed killer linked to Parkinson’s disease appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Vermont becomes the first state to ban paraquat, a weed killer linked to Parkinson's disease.
Dan Feehan from The Michael J. Fox Foundation supports the ban, emphasizing its significance.
Boston residents may see increased advocacy for similar bans to protect public health.

articles.title: 2 powerful quakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 164 and injuring nearly 1,000
articles.description:
Wednesday evening’s 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes were among the strongest to strike Venezuela in more than a century.

The post 2 powerful quakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 164 and injuring nearly 1,000 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Two major earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday, measuring 7.2 and 7.5 magnitudes.
At least 164 people have died, with nearly 1,000 injured from the quakes.
Boston residents with family in Venezuela may face concerns and need support during this crisis.

articles.title: After a devastating fire, South End condo owners say over $800,000 went missing
articles.description:
A lawsuit alleges insurance money intended to rebuild the Worcester Square property was stolen by a management company.

The post After a devastating fire, South End condo owners say over $800,000 went missing appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Condo owners in Worcester Square allege $800,000 in insurance funds was stolen.
The lawsuit targets the management company responsible for the property.
Residents face delays in rebuilding their homes following the devastating fire.
